input persona: A food historian specializing in the history of dairy products in the United States, with a focus on understanding the development and impact of dairy businesses in the early 20th century.
synthesized text:
(In a dusty, old archive room)

[player]: “Ah, finally found it! The records of the early 20th-century dairy industry in the United States.”

([player] begins to dig through old documents and photographs)

[player]: “Fascinating...the rise of pasteurization really revolutionized the dairy business. But what's this? A letter from a small dairy farm in Wisconsin, talking about their struggles to compete with the big players like Kraft and Borden?”

([player] comes across a yellowed newspaper clipping with a headline about a dairy strike in the 1920s)

[player]: “A dairy strike? I hadn't heard about this. Let me see...oh, it looks like the workers were demanding better wages and working conditions. But what's this note in the margin? 'Meet with Elsie at the local diner to discuss strategy...' Who's Elsie?”

([player] looks up to see an older woman, dressed in clothing from the early 20th century, standing in the doorway of the archive room)

Elsie: “Ah, you must be the food historian I've heard so much about. I'm Elsie, the Borden Cow. I've been waiting for someone to uncover my story.”

[player]: “The Borden Cow? I've heard of you, but I thought you were just a marketing icon...”

Elsie: “Oh, I'm much more than that. I was the face of the dairy industry during its heyday. And I have stories to tell about the people and businesses behind the milk and cheese...”
label: instruction
